steps.title: Find the probability assigned to group C
steps.content:
Since every attendee belongs to exactly one group, the probabilities must sum to 1:

$$P(A) + P(B) + P(C) = 1$$

Substituting the given probabilities:

$$0.44 + 0.24 + P(C) = 1$$

$$P(C) = 1 - 0.44 - 0.24 = 0.32$$

steps.title: Multiply the probability by the total to find the count
steps.content:
The number of attendees in group C is the probability times the total number of attendees:

$$\text{Number in C} = 0.32 \times 275$$

$$= \frac{32}{100} \times 275 = \frac{8800}{100} = 88$$

That matches the expected answer — 88 attendees are assigned to group C.
